LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHUNG CHAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, DISMISS PLAINTIFFS CLAIMS, AND STAY THE INSTANT LITIGATION IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT CHUNG CHAN, JR.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. IF MR. CHAN DOES NOT COOPERATE IN OBTAINING RESOLUTION OF THE CLAIMS FILED BY PLAINTIFF TOUR-SARKISSIAN LAW OFFICES, LLC BY ARBITRATION, TSLO IS GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE A MOTION FOR DETERMINATION THAT MR. CHAN HAS WAIVED HIS RIGHT TO ARBITRATION. TLSO HAS NOT SATISFIED ITS "HEAVY BURDEN" THAT MR. CHAN HAS FORFEITED HIS RIGHT TO ARBITRATE TLSO'S CLAIMS. (SAINT AGNES MEDICAL CENTER V. PACIFICARE OF CALIFORNIA (2003) 31 CAL, 4TH 1187, 1195). TLSO HAS NOT SHOWN HOW IT HAS BEEN PREJUDICED BY ANY ACTION OR INACTION OF MR. CHAN. DELAY ALONE, WITHOUT MORE, IS NOT A BASIS FOR A FINDING OF WAIVER ESPECIALLY WHERE, AS HERE, SOME OF THE DELAY COULD REASONABLY BE ATTRIBUTABLE TO TLSO'S OWN INACTION. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant For Order To Show Cause Re Contempt Against Plaintiff, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Defendant
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Phil Foster, Esq. 415-626-7744 of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices for Plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian.
Chuck Chan 510-597-9600 Defendant in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views and makes suggestions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CHUNG CHAN's Motion Of Defendant For Order To Show Cause Re Contempt Against Plaintiff.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Chung W. Chan's motion for an order to show cause re contempt against Plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP is denied. On January 8, 2019, the Court granted Mr. Chan's motion to compel arbitration and stayed this action pending the completion of the arbitration proceedings. The Court has "no jurisdiction to dismiss an arbitration proceeding for failure to prosecute it in a reasonably diligent fashion." (Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1793.) "Appellate courts have . . . routinely rejected parties' efforts to have courts overstep their limited jurisdiction in cases that are stayed pending binding contractual arbitration." (Optimal Markets, Inc. v. Salant (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 912, 924.) Thus, the Court lacks the authority to hold Plaintiff in contempt for purportedly delaying or failing to comply with its order compelling arbitration. (Blake v. Ecker (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 728, 737 [once the trial court stayed plaintiff's civil action, defendants' only avenue for redress when plaintiff failed to timely prosecute the arbitration was in the arbitration proceeding, not through court order].)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Of Order To Show Cause Re Contempt Against Plaintiff, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Defendant.
Off Calendar Mar-04-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration, Dismiss Plaintiffs Claims, And Stay The Instant Litigation In Favor Of Arbitration. Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Defendant
Off Calendar Jan-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chuck Chan, 510-597-9600 of Charles W. Chan Insurance Ageny for Defendant in Pro per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs CHUNG CHAN's Motion To Compel Arbitration, Dismiss Plaintiffs Claims, And Stay The Instant Litigation In Favor Of Arbitration.

Defendant Chung Chan, Jr.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending completion of arbitration proceedings. If Mr. Chan does not cooperate in obtaining resolution of the claims filed by plaintiff Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLC by arbitration, TSLO is given leave to file a motion for determination that Mr. Chan has waived his right to arbitration. TLSO has not satisfied its "heavy burden" that Mr. Chan has forfeited his right to arbitrate TLSO's claims. (Saint Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal, 4th 1187, 1195). TLSO has not shown how it has been prejudiced by any action or inaction of Mr. Chan. Delay alone, without more, is not a basis for a finding of waiver especially where, as here, some of the delay could reasonably be attributable to TLSO's own inaction.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
